sections.heading: Pearls
sections.content:
All patients with suspected ovarian cancer should be referred to a gynecologic oncologist for optimal surgical management, as this is associated with improved survival [51,85,86].
Comprehensive surgical staging is critical for accurate prognosis and treatment planning, as upstaging occurs in ~30% of patients [66-69].
Germline and somatic BRCA testing should be performed early to guide maintenance therapy with PARP inhibitors [OV-C].
Bevacizumab-containing regimens should be withheld 4-6 weeks prior to surgery due to impaired healing [OV-D, 2 of 12].
Platinum hypersensitivity reactions increase with re-exposure; consider desensitization protocols or switch to non-platinum agents [OV-E].
For mucinous ovarian carcinoma, rule out GI primary with evaluation, as treatment may differ [LCOC-4].
Low-grade serous carcinoma is often chemoresistant; hormone therapy and MEK inhibitors are preferred options [LCOC-7, LCOC-8].
In platinum-resistant disease, mirror tumor biomarker testing for targeted therapies, including MSI, TMB, and NTRK fusions [OV-C, OV-D].

cards.text: All patients with suspected ovarian cancer should be referred to a gynecologic oncologist for optimal surgical management, as this is associated with improved survival [51,85,86].
Comprehensive surgical staging is critical for accurate prognosis and treatment planning, as upstaging occurs in ~30% of patients [66-69].
Germline and somatic BRCA testing should be performed early to guide maintenance therapy with PARP inhibitors [OV-C].
Bevacizumab-containing regimens should be withheld 4-6 weeks prior to surgery due to impaired healing [OV-D, 2 of 12].
Platinum hypersensitivity reactions increase with re-exposure; consider desensitization protocols or switch to non-platinum agents [OV-E].
For mucinous ovarian carcinoma, rule out GI primary with evaluation, as treatment may differ [LCOC-4].
Low-grade serous carcinoma is often chemoresistant; hormone therapy and MEK inhibitors are preferred options [LCOC-7, LCOC-8].
In platinum-resistant disease, mirror tumor biomarker testing for targeted therapies, including MSI, TMB, and NTRK fusions [OV-C, OV-D].
